shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Applied to all card-like surfaces (`.lt-shadow`). Soft, diffuse ambient shadow — physically warm, like an object on a desk in indirect daylight. The only elevation primitive in the system.

motion.name: link-hover-color
motion.purpose: Link color change on hover (olive → salmon). Intentionally slow so the warm salmon doesn't feel aggressive on arrival.

motion.name: link-hover-all
motion.purpose: Default link transition (non-color properties). Fast enough to feel responsive.

motion.name: button-hover
motion.purpose: Icon button hover state fill (transparent → outlinegray background). Quick and precise.

motion.name: side-header-reveal
motion.purpose: The annotation border line on section headings expands (width: 0 → 180px) on section hover. The cubic-bezier gives it a deceleration that feels like a pen stroke completing.

motion.name: letter-flip-out
motion.purpose: Each letter exits upward in 3D space. The curve accelerates sharply then decelerates, like a page being flipped.

motion.name: letter-flip-in
motion.purpose: Each letter enters from below (rotateX(-90deg) → 0). Standard ease for a natural landing.

motion.name: cascade-fade-in
motion.purpose: Hero greeting and sub-nav items fade in sequentially. Delays: h1[1] 0.4s, h1[2] 0.8s, li[1-4] 1.3–1.6s. Establishes visual hierarchy on first load.

motion.name: logo-hover
motion.purpose: Logo fades on hover to signal it is interactive (the dark/light theme toggle).

motion.name: prefers-reduced-motion
motion.purpose: All animations and transitions collapse to instant when the user has requested reduced motion. Required for accessibility. Covers AOS data attributes as well.

components.name: IconButton
components.description: Square icon button used in the footer for social links (GitHub, LinkedIn, Instagram, Email, Resume). Transparent background by default; fills with outlinegray on hover. Icon color is muted slate (--light-blue). All buttons are the same 3em × 3em size forming a uniform strip.
components.html:
<button class="ds-icon-btn" aria-label="GitHub" type="button">
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">

  </svg>
</button>

components.name: NavLink
components.description: Horizontal navigation link in the fixed top navbar. 0.75 opacity at rest, underlines on hover using a light gray underline (outlinegray, 2.5px, 5px offset). No background. Dark text (#3d3d3d) even in dark mode so that nav remains legible against the translucent navbar background.
components.html:
• about
  • projects
  • timeline
  • contact

components.name: ProjectCard
components.description: Accordion-style project card. The 2px olive top border is the visual signature of this component — it identifies all project surfaces in the system. Content row shows title (bold) and brief description with an expand chevron. Clicking reveals description paragraph, sub-nav links, and tech chip list. Box shadow is ambient (lt-shadow).
components.html:
Kabo is a karaoke Discord bot developed at Hack the North 2020++ for simulating karaoke with live pitch and lyrical correctness scoring.

  • /github
  • /devpost
  • Python
  • Node.js
  • Discord.js

components.name: TechChip
components.description: Technology tag used inside project accordion panels. Olive fill with light text. No border, no shadow. Used as an unordered list item. Pill-shaped (12px border-radius). Groups related technologies for a project without requiring prose explanation.

components.name: SubNavLink
components.description: Terminal-path navigation link used in both the hero (section navigation) and inside project cards (external links). Monospace font, no list style, horizontal inline-block layout. The slash prefix (e.g. /about, /github) is part of the content — it is the design voice, not a visual decoration. Color inherits from link defaults (olive resting, salmon on hover).

narrative.northStar: The Engineer's Sketchbook — Precise, personal, lived-in. Like a notebook full of careful drawings and personal annotations: both rigorous and warm.
narrative.overview: Austin Luu's portfolio is a personal site that communicates who Austin is (engineer, builder, athlete, food person), showcases range of work, and invites contact. The visual system is built to serve a quick scroll on first visit and a deep read on return. Three audiences: recruiters/hiring managers who scan for signal in under 60 seconds; collaborators/builders who read deeply to understand technical depth and taste; and curious people who follow one thread (e.g., powerlifting + ML) without knowing what they are looking for. The brand personality is curious, technical, warm — and the voice is direct and human. Technical depth comes through in what is shown, not how it is described.
keyCharacteristics: Muted sage primary accent (#84a59d) — a deliberate departure from default blue; calm, grounding, technically restrained
Warm off-white background (#faf8f8) — not clinical white; the slightly warm cast is foundational to the sketchbook feel
Single ambient elevation class (lt-shadow) — soft, diffuse, physically warm; no shadow scales or tokens beyond this
Roboto Mono for terminal-path navigation — the slash-prefixed links (/about, /github) are design voice, not just style
3D letter-flip rotating text — per-letter transform-origin 0.8em Z-axis creates a book-page flip; a technically precise hero moment
Side-header annotation reveal — .side-header::before border expands on section hover via cubic-bezier, like a pen stroke completing
Dracula theme code blocks — a specific aesthetic preference recognizable to engineers, not a generic dark code block
prefers-reduced-motion support baked in — all animations and AOS transforms collapse to instant

rules.name: Olive is primary
rules.body: The olive (#84a59d) accent is the visual signature of the portfolio. It appears on project card top borders, tech chip backgrounds, link resting states, and the side-header annotation border. Do not substitute navy or salmon in these roles.

rules.name: Single elevation primitive
rules.body: The system uses exactly one shadow value (the .lt-shadow class). Do not introduce new shadow tokens, layered shadows, or colored shadows. The ambient philosophy — soft, diffuse, physically warm — is incompatible with sharp or dark shadows.

rules.name: Monospace for terminal paths
rules.body: Roboto Mono is reserved for the sub-nav component. It must not be used for body text, headings, or labels. Its presence signals the terminal-path metaphor that is central to the portfolio's identity voice.

rules.name: Slow hover color transition
rules.body: Link hover color transitions at 0.7s (not 0.2s). The delay is intentional — salmon is a warm color and arriving too quickly would feel aggressive. Respect the timing.

rules.name: Content width cap
rules.body: All main content lives inside 860px max-width centered columns with 5vh 2rem padding. Full-bleed text is not permitted. This is the reading width that supports both quick scan and deep read.

rules.name: Reduced motion override is required
rules.body: Any new animation or transition must be covered by the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Animation collapses to 0.01ms duration, not removal — this preserves the animation-fill-mode: forwards state.

rules.name: No gradient backgrounds
rules.body: The palette is flat. Gradients belong to the startup world this system explicitly rejects. This includes hero overlays, card backgrounds, and button states.

rules.name: Project card top border identifies the surface
rules.body: The 2px olive border-top on .project is the component's visual identity marker. It must not be removed, changed to a different accent color, or replaced with a background fill.

dos: Use olive (#84a59d) as the primary accent on interactive surfaces, borders, and chip backgrounds
Apply the ambient shadow class (.lt-shadow) to all card surfaces
Write navigation links as terminal paths with a leading slash (/about, /github)
Use Roboto Mono exclusively for sub-nav and project link items
Cap content width at 860px with 5vh 2rem padding
Include prefers-reduced-motion overrides for any new animation
Let the 0.7s link hover color transition run its full duration
Use the warm off-white (#faf8f8) background — not pure white (#ffffff)
Show technical depth through what is shown (code snippets, chip lists, project specifics), not through descriptive copy
Keep the navbar translucent (--light-op) so the hero canvas remains visible under scroll
donts: Don't use gradient backgrounds or overlays
Don't introduce new accent colors beyond olive, navy, and salmon
Don't use box-shadows larger, darker, or more numerous than the single .lt-shadow class
Don't write copy with startup superlatives (unleash, empower, transform, revolutionize)
Don't animate layout elements on scroll beyond the existing AOS fade-up on sections
Don't center body text — center alignment is reserved for the footer copyright block only
Don't use Roboto Mono for anything other than the terminal-path navigation metaphor
Don't make the navbar opaque — the translucency is structural, not decorative
Don't remove the 2px olive border-top from project cards
Don't shorten the link hover color transition below 0.5s — the warmth of salmon requires time to land gracefully
